title: Pretrained Language Model in Continual Learning: A Comparative Study

structured.summary: This paper presents a comprehensive empirical study of continual learning (CL) with pretrained language models (PLMs) in NLP, evaluating multiple PLMs, datasets, and CL methods under task- and class-incremental scenarios. It introduces a novel prototype-based layer-wise probing technique to analyze internal forgetting dynamics and demonstrates that experience replay methods outperform regularization and architectural approaches in mitigating catastrophic forgetting.

strengths.point: Comprehensive benchmarking across diverse PLMs, datasets, and CL settings
strengths.evidence: Evaluation involves 5 PLMs, 3 NLP datasets with varying characteristics, and 2 CL scenarios (Task-IL, Class-IL) alongside multiple CL methods.

strengths.point: Novel prototype-based layer-wise probing method revealing internal forgetting patterns
strengths.evidence: The layer-wise analysis uncovers which transformer layers degrade most during CL and how replay methods better preserve internal representations.

weaknesses.point: Limited discussion and comparison to prior work on layer-wise feature analysis in CL
weaknesses.evidence: The paper does not adequately contextualize its probing approach relative to existing transformer layer analysis or representational drift studies.

weaknesses.point: Lack of ablation studies on prototype-based metric and replay buffer sizes
weaknesses.evidence: No sensitivity analysis on prototype selection or detailed experiments varying replay buffer sizes across PLMs and datasets.

questions.question: How sensitive is the prototype-based similarity metric to the choice and number of prototypes?
questions.motivation: Understanding metric robustness is crucial for validating layer-wise conclusions and generalizing the probing approach.

questions.question: How do pretraining objectives of different PLMs influence catastrophic forgetting and replay effectiveness?
questions.motivation: Systematic analysis here could guide PLM selection or adaptation for continual learning.

critique_points.point_001: Limited discussion and comparison to prior work on layer-wise feature analysis in CL. The paper does not adequately contextualize its probing approach relative to existing transformer layer analysis or representational drift studies
critique_points.point_002: Lack of ablation studies on prototype-based metric and replay buffer sizes. No sensitivity analysis on prototype selection or detailed experiments varying replay buffer sizes across PLMs and datasets
